1. **No failure/abort entry in provenance trail** — when run_study raises, the partial trail ends silently with no record of what/why it failed. (HIGH — audit hole)
2. **Failed runs recorded as FINISHED in experiment tracker** — end_run in finally always uses default FINISHED status. (MEDIUM)
3. **run_study spans not correlated to run_id** — no bootstrap_tracing/run_trace_context wrapping, so Phoenix spans can't be tied to the MLflow run. (MEDIUM)
4. **Graph gatenode decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail** — gate_decisions is unchained LangGraph state, contradicting GOVERNANCE §3 "spine." (HIGH/MEDIUM)

findings.title: A raised run leaves a partial provenance trail with no failure entry — the abort reason is unrecoverable from the trail
findings.line: 269-528 (try/finally around the phase machine; finally at 522-527 records nothing)
findings.description: GOVERNANCE.md §3 and the docstring at lines 102-103 explicitly promise that "a caller may pass its own trail to run_study so a run that *raises* (a blocked gate, a firewall violation) still leaves an auditable partial trail." That partial trail is auditable for what *succeeded*, but it records NOTHING about the failure itself: when any phase raises (IngestionError, FirewallViolation, PreRegistrationError, GateError, or an adapter exception), control leaves through the bare try/finally at lines 269/522 and the finally block only calls tracker.end_run — it never records a provenance entry for the abort. PoC against the venv (BadParser raising during INGEST, caller-owned trail): the trail ends at seq 0 'run_start' and seq 1 'phase enter INGEST', then stops. An auditor reading the trail alone cannot distinguish "run aborted at ingest because the parser hit disk corruption" from "run is still in progress" or "run completed but stopped logging" — the single most diagnostic fact (what failed and why) is absent precisely on the failure path the partial-trail feature exists to serve. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of diagnosing a blocked run; a silent truncation is an audit hole, not an audit record.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the phase machine body in try/except/finally: on any exception, before re-raising, record a terminal entry e.g. trail.record('run_failed', f'{type(exc).__name__} during {visited[-1].name if visited else "start"}', error_type=type(exc).__name__, message=str(exc), last_phase=visited[-1].name if visited else None). Keep it inside the chain (so verify_records still passes) and guard the record() call itself so a provenance failure during teardown does not mask the original exception. This makes the partial trail self-describing: it ends with an explicit, chained 'this is why the run stopped' entry.

findings.title: Failed runs are terminated in the experiment tracker as FINISHED — a failed run is indistinguishable from a successful one in the metric store
findings.description: run_study's finally block calls tracker.end_run(run_id) on every exit path, including when a phase raised. MlflowExperimentTracker.end_run calls client.set_terminated(run_id) with no status argument, and MLflow's set_terminated defaults status to "FINISHED" (confirmed from the installed mlflow: the signature is set_terminated(run_id, status=None, ...) and the docstring states it "Defaults to FINISHED"). Result: a run that aborted on a firewall violation or a blocked gate is recorded in the on-box experiment store with status FINISHED, identical to a clean completion. Operators querying the tracking store (the canonical place to see which runs succeeded) cannot tell failed runs from successful ones, and partial metrics logged before the failure sit under a run that looks complete. This defeats the tracker's role as a production run-status surface.
findings.suggested_fix: Distinguish success from failure: capture the outcome in run_study (e.g. set an `ok` flag true only after trail.verify() succeeds) and pass a status into end_run. Add a status parameter to ExperimentTracker.end_run / MlflowExperimentTracker.end_run (default 'FINISHED', pass 'FAILED' on the exception path) so set_terminated(run_id, status=status) marks aborted runs FAILED. The set_terminated call already tolerates the 'KILLED'/'FAILED' RunStatus values.

findings.title: Graph-orchestration gate decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail — the documented “append-only trail” for the durable path is unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state
findings.description: There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE §3 states the chained ProvenanceTrail "is the spine… No step can be retroactively removed or reordered," and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions list "the append-only trail." But the graph path's decisions are NEVER written into the chained ProvenanceTrail: apply_gate_decision takes no trail/recorder and returns a bare dict (PoC confirmed: {'gate_decisions': [{'gate':'review','decision':'rejected','rationale':'no good'}]}), and grep shows no bridge from gate_decisions into ProvenanceTrail (the as_recorder bridge at provenance.py:152 is wired only to the synchronous Gate.evaluate path, not the gatenode). The graph's 'append-only trail' has no prev_hash/entry_hash, no verify_records, and relies entirely on the study correctly declaring a list/add reducer (a documented foot-gun: lines 47-50 warn that a wrong reducer silently drops all but the last decision). So for the durable, cross-process governance path — the one production runs that park for an operator actually use — the most governance-relevant mutations (human approve/reject decisions) are recorded only as unchained, tamper-evident-free state that can be reordered or dropped without detection. The integrity guarantee GOVERNANCE §3 advertises does not hold for this path.
findings.suggested_fix: Bridge graph gate decisions into the chained trail: have make_gate_node (or a thin wrapper) accept a ProvenanceTrail-backed recorder and record each validated decision via trail.record('gate', ...) at apply time, so the durable path's approvals/rejections land in the same hash-chained, verify_records-checkable trail as the synchronous path. At minimum, reconcile the docs to stop calling the unchained gate_decisions list an "append-only trail" with the same integrity as §3, and document that the graph path requires an explicit provenance bridge for a tamper-evident governance record.

findings.title: run_study emits phase/reasoning spans but never starts tracing or stamps run_id baggage — traces cannot be correlated to the run or the experiment-tracker run_id
findings.line: 209, 269-521 (phase spans via tracer.span); compare orchestration/graph.py:104-108 which does call bootstrap_tracing()+run_trace_context()
findings.description: run_study is the documented end-to-end composed runner and it opens trace spans for every phase (tracer.span('phase:INGEST') etc.) and the reasoning seam emits LLM/AGENT spans underneath. But run_study never calls bootstrap_tracing() and never wraps execution in run_trace_context(run_id) — grep of pipeline.py shows no reference to either. run_trace_context (tracing.py:145-163) is what stamps the 'assay.run_id' baggage onto every span so traces are correlatable across stores; the orchestration graph runner (graph.py:104-108) does exactly this, but the primary pipeline does not. Consequence: spans from a run_study execution carry no run_id baggage, so an operator cannot correlate the Phoenix trace for a given run with that run's experiment-tracker run_id (line 539 returns experiment_run_id) or its provenance trail — breaking end-to-end correlation, the explicitly-stated purpose of run_trace_context ("cross-store correlation"). Tracing also depends on something else having called bootstrap_tracing() first, which the composed entrypoint does not guarantee.
findings.suggested_fix: In run_study, after obtaining run_id (or a synthesized correlation id when no tracker is supplied), call bootstrap_tracing() once and wrap the phase machine in `with run_trace_context(corr_id):` so every phase and reasoning span is stamped with the run id and lands at the local collector — mirroring orchestration.graph.run(). Use the experiment_run_id when present so Phoenix spans and the MLflow run share one correlation key.

content.text:
I completed the observability and provenance audit of hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). I read the real code, ran PoCs against the venv, and cross-checked each candidate against GOVERNANCE.md and the ADRs to separate genuine audit gaps from documented scope. Four genuine production-readiness defects, all PoC- or doc-backed:

1. **HIGH — No failure entry in the partial provenance trail** (`/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py`, try/finally at 269/522). The docs promise a raised run leaves an "auditable partial trail," but on any raise the trail truncates silently — no record of what failed or why. PoC: a parser failure during INGEST leaves only `run_start` + `phase enter INGEST`, then nothing.

2. **HIGH — Graph gate decisions never enter the hash-chained trail** (`/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py:44-117`). The durable interrupt/resume path records approve/reject decisions as unchained LangGraph `gate_decisions` state (no `prev_hash`/`entry_hash`, no `verify_records`), yet GOVERNANCE.md calls it "the append-only trail" with §3 integrity. PoC confirms `apply_gate_decision` returns a bare dict with no trail/recorder, and there is no bridge into `ProvenanceTrail`.

3. **MEDIUM — Failed runs recorded as FINISHED in the experiment tracker** (`pipeline.py:522-527` + `observability/tracking.py:84-85`). `end_run` always calls `set_terminated` with the default status (confirmed FINISHED in the installed mlflow), so aborted runs are indistinguishable from successful ones in the metric store.

4. **MEDIUM — `run_study` spans not correlated to the run** (`pipeline.py:209, 269-521`). The composed runner emits phase/reasoning spans but never calls `bootstrap_tracing()` nor wraps execution in `run_trace_context(run_id)` (unlike `orchestration/graph.py:104-108`), so Phoenix traces carry no run_id baggage and can't be tied to the MLflow run or the provenance trail.
